key_issues: BLOCKING: only one provider implementation is tested, so the gen: swap seam (the whole point of a port) is never proven; add a second stub provider and assert both flow through the same validate/result surface.

BLOCKING: 'validation-errors carries a non-empty list' (AC line 96) is unfalsifiable -- structs are plain #:transparent with no guard, so (validation-errors '()) is constructible and nothing rejects it; add a #:guard or document it as a provider contract and test the rejection.

BLOCKING: value matrix is too thin -- only plain numbers (42 ok / 7 error) are validated; JSON null/(json-null), hasheq, list, and string values are never fed, and validation-ok-value round-trip on these is never asserted.
BLOCKING: handle reuse ('called multiple times' -- the literal TS contract) and two-handles-from-one-provider independence are untested; both are classic closure-capture bug surfaces.

Banned-set inconsistency: AC line 100 omits racket/port but Testing Strategy line 141 / checklist line 235 include it; the item-008 shipped helper bans racket/port and passes, so AC line 100 must be corrected.
Closed-set exhaustiveness untested: nothing asserts (validation-result? 42) is #f or that a bare validation-error is not a result; AC line 98's 'exhaustive' claim has no backing test.

SUGGESTED: stub compile behavior on a garbage/invalid schema is unspecified (raise vs defer); a MANY-errors result, a mixed string/integer path, negative-accessor raising, non-jsexpr input, and cross-provider handle behavior are all unexercised.
